content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 ,I Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322
60886 
Ashley K. Dunning 
Nossaman, LLP 
50 California Street, 34th Floor 
San Francisco, CA 94111 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No: A-17-157 
Dear Ms. Dunning: 
August 16, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Marin County Employees' 
Retirement Association ("MCERA") regarding the duties and obligations of one of its appointed 
Board of Retirement members, Dave Shore, regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 We have withdrawn the Section 1090 portion of your question 
per your request. Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Act, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law 
conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code, or Section 1090. We are also not a finder of 
fact when rendering advice (/11 re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide 
assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTION 
1. Does Mr. Shore have a reportable investment interest in Dimensional Fund Advisors 
("Dimensional") given that his ownership interest consists of diversified mutual funds? 
2. Under the Act, must Mr. Shore recuse himself from discussions and voting by the 
MCERA Investment Committee on MCERA's investments in Dimensional because of any of his 
connections with Dimensional? 
CONCLUSION 
I. No. Because the definition of the term "investment" under the Act excludes interests in 
diversified mutual funds registered with the SEC, his interests in Dimensional mutual funds are not 
reportable as investments. 
2. No. Because Mr. Shore does not have an investment interest in, and does not hold a 
business position with Dimensional, he does not nebd to recuse himself from discussions and voting 
by the MCERA Investment Committee. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated, The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 ofTitle 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-17-157 
Page No. 2 
Dave Shore is a board member of MC ERA, as well as a registered financial advisor and part 
owner of Marin Financial Advisors, LLC. 
Dimensional is a global investment manager that provides a range of equity and fixed 
income investment strategies to both institutional investors such as MCERA and private investors. 
The investment vehicles that Dimensional manages are diversified mutual funds registered with the 
Securities & Exchange Commission ("SEC") under the Investment Company Act of 1940. MCERA 
invests in Dimensional's small cap value portfolio. 
On Mr. Shore's Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700) Schedule A-I, he states that he 
owns over $1,000,000 in "various diversified Dimensional Funds, including $120,000 of DFSVX 
which is a strategy that MCERA uses." Mr. Shore reports to MCERA that he also invests in a 
variety of other diversified mutual funds and diversified exchange traded funds that meet the Act's 
requirements for non-reportable investment funds ("ETFs"). In addition, Mr. Shore reports on 
Schedule A-2 of his Form 700 that he owns 10 percent or more of Marin Financial Advisors, LLC 
("Marin Financial"), a registered financial advisory firm, and that he is a "Partner Half Owner" in 
Marin Financial. 
Through Marin Financial, Mr. Shore invests on behalf of his private clients in Dimensional, 
as well as other diversified mutual funds and ETFs, as well. Finally, Mr. Shore also has informed 
MCERA that he serves as an uncompensated member of a Dimensional Sustainability Fund Council 
("Council"), which Dimensional describes as "a group of consultants and wealth managers not 
affiliated with Dimensional working together with Dimensional to research the best ways to provide 
sustainability investment solutions to investors." You note that he is not a "director, officer, partner, 
[or] employee" of Dimensional, and he does not hold any position of management with 
Dimensional or serve as a "trustee" capacity; he simply works with a group of independent 
professionals who collectively provide non-binding recommendations to Dimensional's Board of 
Directors with respect sustainable investing. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. In furtherance of this goal, the Act requires that assets and income of public officials which 
may be materially affected by their official actions should be fully disclosed and in appropriate 
circumstances the officials should be disqualified from acting in order that conflicts of interest may. 
be avoided. (Section 81002(c).) 
Investment Interests in Diversified Muw11l Funds and ETFs: 
Section 82034 provides a definition of the term "investment" under the Act however, the 
definition excludes interests in diversified mutual funds registered with the SEC: 


File No. A-17-157 
Page No. 3 
••Toe tenn •investment' does not include a time or demand deposit in a financial 
institution, shares in a credit union, any insurance policy, interest in a diversified 
mutual fund registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the 
Investment Company Act of 1940 or a common trust fund which is created 
pursuant to Section 1564 of the Financial Code ... " 
Additionally, Regulation 18237 provides: 
.... .  the tenn "investment" does not include a fund, including an exchange 
traded fund (ETF), closed-end fund or fund held in a plan qualified under 
Sections 401 (k), 403(b ), 457 or similar provision of the Internal Revenue 
Code (qualified plan), that is substantially similar to a "diversified mutual 
fund registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the 
Investment Company Act of 1940" if all the following are met: 
44( 1) The fund is a bona fide investment fund that pools money from more 
than 100 investors and invests the money in stocks, bonds, or other 
securities. 
44(2) The fund holds securities of more than 15 issuers. 
"(3) The public official did not influence or control the decision to 
purchase or set I the speci fie fund on behalf of his or her agency during the 
applicable reporting period. 
"( 4) The public official does not influence or control the selection of any 
specific investment purchased and sold on behalf of the fund. 
"(5) The fund does not have a stated policy of concentrating its holdings in 
the same industry or business. 
44(b) For purposes of subdivisions (a)(l) and (a)(2), an ETF, clos©d-end 
fund or qualified plan is presumed to have more than 100 investors and 
hold securities of more than 15 issuers." 
Pursuant to the specific language of Section 82034, the tenn "investment" does not include 
an interest in a diversified mutual fund registered with the SEC and you have stated that his 
investments in ETFs meet the criteria for being exempted from the definition of income pursuant to 
the guidelines in Regulation 1823 7. 
Conflicts of Interest 
Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one 
or more of the public official's financial interests. Section 87103 also sets forth the interests from 
which a conflict of interest may arise under the Act. They include: 


File No. A-17-157 
Page No. 4 
1. Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect 
investment worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section 
87103(a).) 
2. Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending 
institution made in the regular course of business on terms available to 
the public without regard to official status, aggregating five hundred 
dollars ($500) or more in value provided or promised to, received by, 
the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the 
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
3. Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, 
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. 
(Section 87103(d).) 
Interests related to Marin Financial Advisors, LLC: 
As a registered financial advisor and part owner of Marin Financial Advisors, LLC, it is 
both a business interest under Sections 87103(a) and (d) and a source of income under Section 
87103(c). Both Marin Financial Advisors, LLC and any clients of the business are considered 
sources of income. 2 
Interests related to the Council 
Section 82005 defines "business entity" as "any organization or enterprise operated for a 
profit, including but not limited to a proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture, 
syndicate, corporation or association." 
Section 87103(d) provides that a public official has a potentially disqualifying conflict of 
interest in a business entity in which the official is "a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or 
holds a position of management" in the business entity. Given the above referenced description of 
the Cpuncil, you assert that Mr. Shore does not "hold a position" in Dimensional, as provided in 
Section 87103( d) so as to have an "interest" in that entity, because he is not a "director, officer, 
partner, [or] employee" of Dimensional, and he does not hold any position of management with 
Dimensional. Further, Mr. Shore reports to MCERA that he is not in a "trustee" role with respect to 
Dimensional as a result of his work on the Council or otherwise. Rather, he simply works with a 
group of independent professionals who collectively provide non-binding recommendations to 
Dimensional 's Board of Directors with respect sustainable investing. 
Based on the facts provided, Mr. Shore does not have an investment interest in, as defined 
by Section 82034, ilor does he hold a business position with, Dimensional lfund Advisors; and 
would not need to recuse himself from decisions involving Dimensional. 
! We have not analyzed potential conflicts of interest related to clients who are sources of income because you 
have not provided facts pertaining to any clients. 


Foreseeability 
File No. A-17-157 
Page No. 5 
The standard for foreseeability differs depending on whether or not an interest is explicitly 
involved in the decision. (Regulation 18701.) Where the interest is explicitly involved, a financial 
effect is presumed reasonably foreseeable. None of Mr. Shore's interests related to Marin Financial 
Advisors, LLC are explicitly involved in the decisions. 
Where the financial interest is not explicitly involved in a decision, such as here, the 
foreseeability standard is whether the financial effect can be recognized as a "realistic possibility· 
and more than hypothetical or theoretical." (Regulation 18701 (b).) 
Materiality 
Investments in Business Entities and sources of Income: Regulation 18702.1 (b) provides 
that an effect on a business entity (including a business entity that is a source of income) 
is material as follows: 
"[The] financial effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient information 
would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would 
contribute to a change in the price of the business entity's publicly traded stock, or 
the value of a privately-held business entity." 
Nothing in your facts suggest that the decisions made by MCERA will contribute to a 
change in the value of Marin Financial Advisors, LLC, therefore there is no reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect upon this interest and no potential conflict for Mr. Shore with respect to 
this interest. 
Therefore, Mr. Shore does not have a financial interest under the Act in the diversified 
mutual funds and ETFs in which he has investments and these interests would not cause a conflict 
of interest for him should he participate in decisions before the MCERA board that relate to these 
funds. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
